title: 10 best herbal essential oils for stye
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for stye are natural remedies that help alleviate symptoms and promote healing for this painful condition. These oils are derived from plants and contain potent compounds that combat bacterial and fungal infections, reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair. The best herbal essential oils for stye include Calendula officinalis, Melaleuca alternifolia, Aloe barbadensis, and others, such as Lavandula angustifolia, Eucalyptus globulus, Echinacea purpurea, Silybum marianum, Hypericum perforatum, Aloe vera, and Geranium maculatum. If left untreated, stye can cause discomfort, blurred vision, and even lead to more severe eye infections, affecting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower essential oils helps with stye because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as echinacoside and chlorogenic acid, work to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that cause styes, promoting a speedy recovery. Additionally, the oil's soothing and calming effects help to relieve discomfort and itching associated with styes, providing fast relief and reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial activity: Echinacea contains compounds like cichoric acid and caffeic acid, which have been shown to exhibit antibacterial properties, helping to combat bacterial infections associated with stye.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Echinacea's polyphenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and redness in the affected area, making it easier to manage stye symptoms.

Immunomodulatory activity: Echinacea has been shown to modulate the immune system, which may help boost the body's natural defenses against infections that can cause stye, reducing the severity of symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the purple coneflower plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with stye.
Barks: The barks of the purple coneflower plant have antibacterial properties, which help combat bacterial infections that can cause stye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea flowers in the morning when dew is still present for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested flowers using a clean towel and spread them evenly on paper bags for three days.
Use a crock-pot or slow cooker to infuse dried echinacea purpurea flowers with 1 cup of carrier oil such as coconut oil at a ratio of 1:2.
Steep the mixture in the crock-pot for 24 hours allowing the herbal properties to extract into the carrier oil.
Filter the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard solids before transferring the echinacea essential oil to dark glass bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with stye because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil's active compounds, such as calendulin and flavonoids, effectively soothe redness and swelling associated with styes. Its antiseptic properties also help to prevent bacterial growth, promoting a faster healing process. By applying the oil topically or adding it to a warm compress, pot marigold essential oil can help alleviate discomfort, reduce the size of the stye, and promote healthy drainage, ultimately speeding up recovery from this common eye infection.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Pot Marigold essential oil has antibacterial properties that help combat bacterial infections, which can cause stye by reducing inflammation and killing off bacteria that contribute to the infection.

Anti-inflammatory: The essential oil also exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and redness associated with styes, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing and calming irritated skin.

Antioxidant: Pot Marigold essential oil has antioxidant properties that help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the formation of stye and other eye infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and pain associated with styes.
Flowers: The flowers are rich in antioxidants and antimicrobial properties, making them effective against bacterial infections causing styes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers at dawn or early morning for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer on paper towels for three days.
Place dried calendula flowers in a clean glass jar and cover them with high-proof vodka or everclear.
Allow the mixture to steep for 14-21 days, shaking the jar daily, to release the oils.
Filter the liquid through cheesecloth into another glass container, discarding the solids and preserving the calendula essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Germander essential oils helps with stye because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, germander oil can help reduce swelling and redness associated with sty, promoting healing and alleviating discomfort. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to the development of a stye. By reducing inflammation and fighting infection, germander essential oils can provide natural relief from the painful and unsightly symptoms of a stye.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Herbal germander essential oil has been shown to exhibit strong antibacterial activity, which helps to combat the bacterial infections that often cause styes and reduce their severity.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Germander oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and redness associated with styes, promoting a faster recovery and reducing discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal germander essential oil is rich in antioxidants that can help neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative damage to the skin, which may contribute to the development of styes or exacerbate their symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Germander contain bioactive compounds like sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in treating stye.
Barks: The barks of Germander contain phenolic compounds and terpenoids that exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, helping to reduce inflammation and prevent infection in stye.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried teucrium chamaedrys leaves and store them in an airtight container for future use.
Combine 10 grams of the dried leaves with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or grapeseed oil.
Steep the mixture for 24 hours allowing the herbs to infuse into the oil at room temperature.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Allow the oil to mature for 48 hours before transferring it to a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid for storage and future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe essential oils helps with stye because it possesses antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and soothing properties that can effectively combat the underlying infection causing the stye. The aloe vera gel helps to reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with styes, while also promoting healing and speeding up the natural removal process. Additionally, its antiseptic properties help prevent further bacterial growth and infection, allowing the stye to clear up quickly and naturally.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with styes, allowing them to heal faster and more effectively.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of aloe vera essential oil help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause styes, preventing further infection and promoting healing.
Wound-healing: Aloe vera's wound-healing properties promote the repair and regeneration of skin tissues, helping to quickly resolve stye lesions and reduce scarring.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them an effective choice for soothing and reducing redness associated with styes.
Roots: The roots contain powerful antioxidants that help combat bacterial infections and promote healing of the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves and trim them into small pieces for extraction.
Combine 20 grams of chopped aloe vera with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Steep the mixture in a dark glass container at room temperature for 24 hours to allow infusion.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard solids after 30 minutes preparation time.
Bottle the herbal aloe vera essential oil and store it in an airtight container in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort essential oils helps with stye because of its potent antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. When applied topically to the affected area, it helps to reduce swelling and redness, while also combating the underlying infection that caused the stye in the first place. Its antimicrobial properties also aid in preventing further bacterial growth, promoting a faster healing process and reducing the likelihood of complications.

Antioxidant: The herb's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals that may exacerbate inflammation and tissue damage caused by the sty, promoting faster healing and reducing scarring.

Wound-healing and soothing: St. John's Wort has been traditionally used to promote wound healing and soothe skin irritations. Its topical application can help accelerate the recovery process of a sty by reducing discomfort, redness, and swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used to treat chronic and stubborn cases of stye, the root of St John's Wort is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain.
Flowers: The flowers of St John's Wort are rich in flavonoids and anthraquinones, which have antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections causing stye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves according to local regulations and guidelines.
Dry the plant material using a combination of air drying and gentle heat for 3-4 weeks.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or food processor.
Mix the powdered plant material with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a ratio of 1:5, allowing it to steep for 2-3 weeks.
Filter the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the liquid extract from the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with stye because of its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and soothing properties. When applied topically, chamomile oil can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with a stye. Its antimicrobial properties can also combat bacterial infections that may be causing the stye in the first place. Antibacterial: The antibacterial properties of chamomile oil can help combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to the development of a stye, promoting healing and preventing further infection.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality matricaria chamomilla flowers and dry them properly for at least three days.
Combine one cup of dried chamomile flowers with four cups of carrier oil like sweet almond or jojoba oil.
Steep the mixture in a dark glass jar for six to eight weeks, shaking daily for better infusion.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container and discard solids.
Store the resulting essential oil in a cool, dark place for up to one year, using it as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle essential oils helps with stye because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The oil's active compounds, silymarin and silibinin, help to reduce redness, swelling, and pus formation associated with styes. remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silymarin, a powerful flavonoid with anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with styes.
Seeds: High in omega-3 fatty acids and antioxidants, which help soothe and calm the skin around the eye area, promoting healthy healing of stye infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh silybum marianum flowers in the morning when they are fully open.
Dry the harvested flowers in a clean, dark place for at least 2 weeks or until completely dry.
Combine 20g of dried silybum marianum flowers with 100ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a glass container.
Steep the mixture in a warm location away from direct sunlight for 6 to 8 hours or overnight.
Strain and filter the resulting essential oil using cheesecloth or coffee filters before transferring it to dark glass bottles.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems, taking care not to touch the plants with bare skin.
Cleanly dry the harvested plant material to prevent contamination and mold growth.
Cut the dried plant material into small pieces or grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 1 cup of the powdered plant material with 5 cups of boiling water, allowing the mixture to steep for at least 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter, discarding the solids and collecting the herbal infusion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion essential oils helps with stye because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The oil's natural compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and triterpenoids, work together to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with styes. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of dandelion essential oil help to combat bacterial infections that can cause styes. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of taraxacum officinale and rinse them gently with distilled water.
Steam distill the leaves using a steam distillation apparatus at atmospheric pressure for 4-6 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with an inert lid, such as argon or nitrogen, at room temperature.
Use the essential oil topically by mixing 2-3 drops with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, for treatment of stye.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow essential oils helps with stye because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The oil's active compounds, such as chamazulene and azulene, penetrate deep into the skin, reducing swelling and redness associated with sty eyes. Additionally, yarrow essential oil's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial overgrowth, which can contribute to stye formation. By applying a few drops of yarrow essential oil to a cotton pad, you can gently massage it onto the affected area, promoting natural healing and alleviating discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory compounds in yarrow essential oil can reduce swelling and pain associated with styes, promoting healing and alleviating discomfort.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make medicinal essential oils for stye because they contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

Flowers: The flowers are used to make medicinal essential oils for stye because they contain a high concentration of linalool, which has antibacterial and antifungal properties that can help combat bacterial and fungal infections causing stye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves at dawn in mid-summer when the plant is in full bloom.
Dry the harvested leaves in small batches using a dehydrator set to low temperature for 24 hours.
Steam distill 100g of dried achillea millefolium leaves with 500ml of water for 30 minutes using a steam distillation apparatus.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle within 1 hour after distillation.
Store the essential oil in an amber glass bottle away from direct sunlight and heat for up to 6 months.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat stye with herbal essential oils is by applying a warm compress infused with tea tree oil to reduce swelling and combat bacteria. Mix 5-7 drops of tea tree oil with 1 tablespoon of coconut or olive oil and apply it to the affected area for 10 minutes, twice a day. Repeat this process until the stye resolves.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to stye that are treated with herbal essential oils are conjunctivitis, pink eye, and blepharitis. Tea tree oil is often used to treat bacterial conjunctivitis, while calendula oil helps soothe and calm irritated eyes. Geranium oil can also be used to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes. These oils work by reducing bacteria and promoting antibacterial properties, making them effective natural remedies for these common eye ailments.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis essential oils helps with stye because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. It has the ability to reduce swelling and ease pain associated with styes. The oil's antiseptic properties help to combat bacterial infections that cause styes, promoting a faster healing process. Additionally, calendula oil's soothing and calming effects can help to reduce redness and discomfort, making it an effective natural remedy for stye treatment. This natural approach can help to alleviate symptoms and promote a healthy recovery.
remedy_constituents: Carotenoids: These antioxidant compounds help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area, thus alleviating the symptoms of a stye.
Flavonoids: These phenolic compounds possess anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain associated with styes, promoting a faster recovery.
Triterpenoids: These compounds exhibit antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections, such as those caused by Staphylococcus aureus, which can lead to stye formation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most widely used part due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds.
Leaves: Leaves are also used to make essential oils, as they contain similar properties to the flowers and can aid in reducing inflammation.
Stems: Stems are sometimes used to make essential oils, but less frequently than flowers and leaves, as they may contain lower concentrations of beneficial compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh calendula officinalis flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer on paper towels for 2-3 weeks.
Steam distill the dried flowers with a ratio of 1 part plant material to 2 parts water for 4 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and separate it from the water using a separating funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter paper into a dark glass bottle for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils helps with stye because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as cymene and geraniol, have been shown to combat bacterial overgrowths that cause styes. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils promote a healthy environment for the eyelid glands, allowing them to function properly and reducing the likelihood of stye formation. This natural remedy can help alleviate stye symptoms and promote overall eye health. Regular application can lead to quick relief.
remedy_constituents: Cymene: Helps to reduce inflammation and fight bacterial infections that cause stye.
Limonene: Exhibits antimicrobial properties that help to combat the underlying bacterial cause of stye.
Cineole (Eucalyptol): Possesses anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to reduce the severity and duration of stye.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil due to their high concentration of active compounds like cineole.
Buds: Melaleuca alternifolia bud essential oil is used for its antimicrobial properties, which can help in treating stye by reducing bacterial growth.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Melaleuca alternifolia are also used to make essential oil, which is often used in combination with leaf oil to enhance its therapeutic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melaleuca leaves at the right stage of growth when the plant is in full bloom and the leaves are a vibrant green color.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm place with good air circulation to reduce moisture content to 10% within 7 days.
Steam distill the dried leaves using a steam distillation apparatus at a temperature range of 80-100°c for 3-4 hours.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation apparatus by separating it from the water and plant material using a condenser and separator.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any remaining impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis essential oils helps with stye because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The oil's high concentration of vitamins A, C, and E, as well as its fatty acids, work together to soothe and calm the skin. It helps to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with styes, promoting a faster recovery. Additionally, the oil's antimicrobial properties help to prevent further bacterial growth, allowing the affected area to heal quickly and naturally. This makes Aloe barbadensis essential oil a popular natural remedy for stye treatment.
remedy_constituents: Furanocoumarins: They help with stye by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, which reduce bacterial infections that cause the sty.
Phenolic compounds: They help with stye by acting as anti-inflammatory agents, reducing swelling and redness associated with the sty.
Triterpenoids: They help with stye by promoting wound healing and reducing inflammation, which accelerates the recovery process from a sty.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high content of aloe vera gel, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature aloe barbadensis leaves from well-drained soil and store them in a cool dry place for 2 weeks.
Wash the aloe barbadensis leaves with distilled water to remove dirt and bacteria then pat them dry with a clean towel.
Cut the aloe barbadensis leaves into small pieces and extract the gel using a food processor with a 1:1 ratio for 30 minutes.
Transfer the aloe barbadensis gel to a distillation apparatus and heat it to 212°f with a distillation ratio of 0.5:1 for 2 hours.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation apparatus using a sterile glass container and store it in a cool dark place for 30 days.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with stye because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil's ability to combat bacterial and fungal infections can aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healing of the stye. The antiseptic properties of the oil can also help prevent further infection, allowing the stye to resolve on its own. remedy_constituents: Linalool: This monoterpene alcohol has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the redness and swelling associated with a stye.
Linalyl acetate: This ester has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial infections that may cause or exacerbate a stye.
Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing of the affected area.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used due to their high concentration of linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help combat stye.
Leaves: Used for their similar composition to flowers, providing a similar set of benefits to treat stye.
Stems: Not a primary choice, however, it may be used in some blends due to the presence of a small amount of essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature flowers of lavandula angustifolia when they are in full bloom and have turned a light purple color.
Clean and dry the harvested flowers by spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried flowers using a steam distillation apparatus with a 1:3 flower-to-water ratio for 3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil by condensing the steam into a collecting vessel for 30 minutes.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities and sediment for 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with stye because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's ability to combat bacterial and fungal infections helps to reduce the severity and duration of stye. Limonene: Demonstrates anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, contributing to the reduction of swelling and the prevention of further infection in the affected area.

Barks: Utilized for their antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which aid in preventing infection and promoting healing of the stye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in the early morning when the dew is still present on the leaves.
Dry the harvested leaves in a well-ventilated area for 2-3 days to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Steam distill 100g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves with 200ml of distilled water for 3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a sterile cotton pad to remove any impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils helps with stye because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid that help reduce redness and swelling associated with stye. remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 grams of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves at peak blooming time.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer at 35-40 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried plant material with 100 grams of distilled water at 90-100 degrees celsius.
Separate the distilled essential oil from the water using a separatory funnel and collect 1-2 milliliters.
Filter the collected essential oil through a 0.2-micron filter to remove impurities and store in a dark glass bottle.

remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum flowers and leaves when they are in full bloom and dry them immediately to preserve quality.
Steam distill the dried flowers and leaves at 100-110°c for 3-4 hours to obtain the essential oil.
Collect the distillate and filter it using a coffee filter to remove impurities and debris.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to protect it from light and air.
Label the bottle with the date of distillation the percentage of essential oil and any safety precautions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum essential oils helps with stye because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The antiseptic properties of St. John's Wort essential oil help to speed up the healing process and prevent further complications. By reducing the redness and swelling, it creates a conducive environment for the stye to heal naturally and quickly.
remedy_constituents: N-hydroxyhyperforin: It helps to reduce inflammation associated with stye, promoting healing and soothing of the affected area.
Hyperforin: It exhibits antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent the spread of infection and reduce the severity of stye symptoms.
Quercetin: It has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with stye, promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Flowers: These are the primary source of essential oil for St. John's Wort, as they contain the highest concentration of bioactive compounds.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant also contain essential oil and are often used as a secondary source, particularly when flowers are not available.
Stems: The stems of Hypericum perforatum may be used to produce a more subtle, yet still beneficial essential oil for treating stye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the hypericum perforatum flowers in their peak potency during the summer months for maximum potency.
Steam distill the fresh hypericum perforatum flowers in a 10 to 1 water to plant ratio for 2 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities.
Store the filtered hypericum perforatum essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight lid immediately.
Label and date the hypericum perforatum essential oil bottle and store it in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera essential oils helps with stye because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that reduce swelling and fight infection. The oil's soothing and moisturizing properties also calm the irritated eye area, promoting a faster recovery. Additionally, aloe vera's ability to penetrate deep into the skin helps to break down the pus and debris that can cause a stye to form, ultimately helping to clear up the infection and prevent future occurrences. Regular use can lead to a faster healing process.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with stye.
Aloe-bergenin: Aloe-bergenin has antibacterial properties, which help combat the underlying bacterial infection causing the stye.
Cinnamic acid: Cinnamic acid has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce pain, inflammation, and bacterial growth associated with stye.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain a thick, gel-like substance that can be extracted to create aloe vera essential oil, which is used to treat stye due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 15-20 mature aloe vera leaves from a clean and dry environment for maximum potency.
Cut the leaves into small pieces and extract the gel using a fine mesh strainer for 10 minutes.
Mix the aloe vera gel with 100ml of carrier oil such as jojoba oil in a 1:10 ratio.
Steep the mixture in a warm water bath at 60-70 degree celsius for 2-3 hours to activate the properties.
Strain and bottle the herbal essential oil and store it in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Geranium maculatum essential oils helps with stye because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains geraniol, a compound that inhibits the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms, which often cause stye. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and redness associated with stye. By promoting a healthy environment and reducing inflammation, Geranium maculatum essential oils can help alleviate stye symptoms and speed up the healing process, providing relief from discomfort and promoting overall eye health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with stye.
Phenolic acids: They have antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial infections that cause stye.
Triterpenoids: They exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help soothe and heal the affected area of the stye.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to create essential oils for their antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacterial infections that cause sty.
Roots: The roots are used to create essential oils that contain compounds with anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain associated with sty.
Flowers: The flowers are used to create essential oils that contain compounds with antiseptic properties, which help prevent infection and promote healing of the sty.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh geranium maculatum roots and leaves in early summer when the plant is in full bloom.
Steam distill the harvested plant material for 3-4 hours at a temperature between 100-150 degrees fahrenheit.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the condensed water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities and debris.
Store the extracted geranium maculatum essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.
